summary: Ukrainian drones penetrated Moscow's air defenses and struck the Kapotnya oil refinery, while Russian forces hit the Zaturovo refinery in Poltava and a fuel depot in Boryspol. Multiple UAV groups are reported moving across northern and eastern fronts, including from Chernihiv toward Kyiv and from Kherson toward Mykolaiv. International support includes Belgium's donation of seven F‑16 jets and the UK’s pledge of 150,000 drones by year‑end.
key_points: Ukrainian drones breached Russian air defenses and attacked the Kapotnya oil refinery near Moscow, a key fuel facility (Source: @UkraineNow/82010).
Russian forces struck the Zaturovo oil refinery in Poltava and a fuel storage site in Boryspol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30995).
A group of UAVs moved from Chernihiv region northward along the Belarus border toward Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65541).
UAVs from Kherson region headed toward Mykolaiv city (Source: @kpszsu/65554).
Belgium announced donation of seven F‑16 fighter jets to Ukraine, three for operational use (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31003).
The UK pledged 150,000 drones, 350 air‑defence missiles and radars to Ukraine by year‑end (Source: @UkraineNow/82032).
German defense ministry confirmed $400 million for Ukrainian air‑defence systems and Patriot missiles (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30996).
Russian tactical aviation activity reported on the eastern direction, indicating potential air‑strike threats (Source: @kpszsu/65552).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian drones successfully penetrated Moscow’s layered air defence and struck the Kapotnya oil refinery, a critical fuel hub near the capital. Russian forces responded with strikes on the Zaturovo refinery in Poltava and a fuel depot in Boryspol, highlighting ongoing strategic targeting of energy infrastructure. International assistance is expanding, with Belgium delivering F‑16 jets and the UK pledging large numbers of drones and air‑defence assets.

points: Ukrainian UAVs from the north‑eastern Chernihiv area moved along the Belarus border toward the Kyiv region, indicating reconnaissance or strike preparation (Source: @kpszsu/65541).
A UAV group from Kherson headed toward Mykolaiv city, suggesting a focus on southern port areas (Source: @kpszsu/65554).
Multiple UAV clusters were reported moving from Sumy toward Shostka, Khutir‑Mykhaylivskyi, and from Kharkiv toward Chuhuiv and Shevchenkove (Source: @kpszsu/65554).
Ukrainian drones attacked a railway bridge over the North‑Crimean Canal near Rozdolne in occupied Crimea (Source: @UkraineNow/82011).
Ukrainian forces claimed a successful strike on Moscow’s Kapotnya oil refinery, a key fuel supplier for the capital (Source: @UkraineNow/82010).
Ukrainian drones reportedly hit an energy object in Poltava region, damaging equipment and injuring one civilian (Source: @UkraineNow/82013).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Odessa from the sea area (Source: @kpszsu/65538).
Russian forces struck the Zaturovo oil refinery in Poltava and a fuel storage facility in Boryspol (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30995).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the eastern direction, warning of possible air‑strike operations (Source: @kpszsu/65552).
Seven Ukrainian UAVs were reportedly destroyed over Kaluga region at night (Source: @tass_agency/381388).
Russian authorities reported a gas explosion incident in Novosibirsk region, unrelated to combat (Source: @tass_agency/381380).
Russian air units Tu‑95MS were observed taking off from Olenya airfield, purpose unspecified (Source: @eRadarrua/97237).
Russian forces claimed liberation of Ray‑Alexandrovka in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30999).
Russian drones were reportedly used in intensified attacks on civilian targets in Zaporizhzhia, per Ukrainian intelligence (Source: @UkraineNow/82027).
UAVs from Sumy region moved toward Shostka, Khutir‑Mykhaylivskyi, and from Kharkiv toward Chuhuiv and Shevchenkove (Source: @kpszsu/65554).
A UAV group in the north‑west of Chernihiv headed south, while another in Konotop district moved west (Source: @kpszsu/65526).
Five UAVs from Bryansk were reported heading to Horodnia in Chernihiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/97215).
Six UAVs were observed circling the border of Bryansk and Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/97219).
UAV activity was noted in the southern part of Mykolaiv heading toward Kobyleve (Source: @kpszsu/65558).
UAVs from Sumy region were seen moving toward Sumy city, Shostka, and Yampil (Source: @kpszsu/65558).
UAVs from Kharkiv’s southern area moved toward Kozelshchyna (Source: @kpszsu/65558).
UAVs on the north of Chernihiv moved toward the city of Chernihiv (Source: @kpszsu/65551).
A UAV group from Bryansk entered Chernihiv region, heading toward Horodnia (Source: @eRadarrua/97215).
Three UAVs were reported circling over the border of Bryansk and Chernihiv (Source: @eRadarrua/97219).
UAVs from the north‑west of Chernihiv moved southward (Source: @kpszsu/65526).
A UAV cluster was observed moving from the border area of Belarus toward Slavutych and Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/65557).
Three Shahed missiles were tracked from Chernihiv northward toward Desnyansky district of Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67899).
A Shahed missile flew from Chernihiv toward Kyiv/Borispol (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67901).
UAVs from Kherson region headed toward Mykolaiv city (Source: @kpszsu/65554).
UAVs from southern Mykolaiv region moved toward Kobyleve (Source: @kpszsu/65558).
UAVs from the Black Sea area were directed toward Odessa (Source: @kpszsu/65538).
Ukrainian drones struck a railway bridge in occupied Crimea near Rozdolne (Source: @UkraineNow/82011).
Russian forces launched 7 ballistic missiles and 239 drones across Ukraine, with hits on multiple locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82014).
Ukrainian drones attacked energy infrastructure in Poltava, causing injuries and damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82013).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Zaporizhzhia heading from the south toward the city (Source: @eRadarrua/97239).
Ukrainian drones breached Moscow’s air defence and struck the Kapotnya oil refinery (Source: @UkraineNow/82010).
Seven Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Kaluga region (Source: @tass_agency/381388).
German defense ministry confirmed $400 million for Ukrainian Patriot air‑defence systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30996).
Belgium announced donation of seven F‑16 fighters, three for operational use (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31003).
The UK pledged 150,000 drones, 350 air‑defence missiles and radars to Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/82032).
Russian tactical aviation activity reported on the eastern front, indicating potential strike threats (Source: @kpszsu/65552).
Tu‑95MS bombers were observed taking off from Olenya airfield (Source: @eRadarrua/97237).

key_developments: Ukrainian UAVs penetrated Moscow’s layered air defence and struck the Kapotnya oil refinery, a critical fuel hub near the capital (Source: @UkraineNow/82010).
Russian forces hit the Zaturovo oil refinery in Poltava and a fuel storage depot in Boryspol, targeting Ukrainian energy assets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30995).

Multiple UAV groups are active across northern and eastern fronts, moving from Chernihiv toward Kyiv and from Sumy toward Shostka, indicating coordinated reconnaissance and strike preparation (Sources: @kpszsu/65541, @kpszsu/65554, @kpszsu/65526).
Belgium will provide seven F‑16 fighter jets to Ukraine, enhancing its air‑combat capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31003).
The United Kingdom pledged 150,000 drones, 350 air‑defence missiles and radars to Ukraine by year‑end, expanding its UAV and air‑defence inventory (Source: @UkraineNow/82032).
Germany allocated $400 million for Ukrainian Patriot air‑defence systems, strengthening Ukraine’s missile‑defence posture (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30996).
Russian tactical aviation activity on the eastern direction signals potential escalation of air‑strike operations against front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/65552).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian strikes on Ukrainian energy facilities, including the Zaturovo refinery and Boryspol fuel depot, aim to degrade Ukraine's logistical capacity. Ukrainian UAV incursions into Russian airspace, notably the Kapotnya refinery attack, demonstrate growing offensive reach and pose a strategic threat to Russian fuel supplies. Continued Russian tactical aviation activity on the eastern front suggests preparation for intensified air operations, while Ukraine's expanding UAV fleet, supported by Western aid, increases pressure on Russian rear areas.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are augmenting Ukraine's air capabilities, with Belgium delivering F‑16 jets and the UK supplying large numbers of drones and air‑defence missiles. Germany's financial commitment to Patriot systems further bolsters Ukraine's defensive posture. These contributions aim to counter Russian air and missile threats and support Ukrainian offensive UAV operations across multiple fronts.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV movement patterns show coordinated groups shifting from border regions toward strategic targets, indicating a command‑and‑control network for drone operations. Russian air‑defence interceptions over Kaluga demonstrate effective detection but also highlight vulnerabilities in deeper Russian airspace. The influx of Western air assets and munitions will likely shift the balance in aerial engagements, while Russian tactical aviation readiness on the eastern front warrants close monitoring for potential escalation.
